analysis.reasoning: Core message: Cites an EU official urging cooperation and multilateralism on COVID-19 and rejecting blame terms like “Chinese virus.” It relays a Chinese spokesperson amplifying this stance. Evaluation: CH1 (West immoral/hostile): Not promoted; no attack on the West, only rejection of blame rhetoric. CH2 (China is a benevolent power): Implicitly aligns with multilateralism and cooperation—values central to CH2 and often used to present China as cooperative and opposed to recrimination. Though framed via the EU quote, the Chinese spokesperson’s amplification promotes this cooperative, multilateral stance. CH3 (epic history): Not present. CH4 (appropriate political system/values, domestic): Not addressed. CH5 (CCP succeeds): Not addressed. CH6 (culture/heritage): Not relevant. Conclusion: The tweet promotes CH2.
